heading: Authority to conduct inspections
label: 6723 Authority to conduct inspections

B, title XI, § 1117]113 Stat. 1536Nov. 29, 1999“(a).—ReimbursementDuring the 2-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [], the United States National Authority (as designated pursuant to section 101 of the Chemical Weapons Convention Implementation Act of 1998 [] (as contained in division I of )) shall, upon request of the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, reimburse the Federal Bureau of Investigation for all costs incurred by the Bureau for such period in connection with implementation of section 303(b)(2)(A) of that Act [], except that such reimbursement may not exceed $2,000,000 for such 2-year period.Nov. 29, 199922 U.S.C. 6711Public Law 105–27722 U.S.C. 6723(b)(2)(A)“(b).—ReportNot later than 180 days prior to the expiration of the 2-year period described in subsection (a), the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall prepare and submit to the Committee on International Relations [now Committee on Foreign Affairs] of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate a report on how activities under section 303(b)(2)(A) of the Chemical Weapons Convention Implementation Act of 1998 [] will be fully funded and implemented by the Federal Bureau of Investigation notwithstanding the expiration of the 2-year period described in subsection (a).”22 U.S.C. 6723(b)(2)(A)

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: a Prohibition
children.text: No inspection of a plant, plant site, or other facility or location in the United States shall take place under the Convention without the authorization of the United States National Authority in accordance with the requirements of this subchapter.

children.heading: Technical Secretariat inspection teams
children.label: 1 Technical Secretariat inspection teams
children.text: Any duly designated member of an inspection team of the Technical Secretariat may inspect any plant, plant site, or other facility or location in the United States subject to inspection pursuant to the Convention.

children.label: A
children.text: a special agent of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, as designated by the Federal Bureau of Investigation, accompanies each inspection team visit pursuant to paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: no employee of the Environmental Protection Agency or the Occupational Safety and Health Administration accompanies any inspection team visit conducted pursuant to paragraph (1); and

children.label: C
children.text: the number of duly designated representatives shall be kept to the minimum necessary.

children.heading: Objections to individuals serving as inspectors
children.label: 3 Objections to individuals serving as inspectors
i
children.text: such individual is or has been a member of, or a participant in, any group or organization that has engaged in, or attempted or conspired to engage in, or aided or abetted in the commission of, any terrorist act or activity;

children.label: ii
children.text: such individual has committed any act or activity which would be a felony under the laws of the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the participation of such individual as a member of an inspection team would pose a risk to the national security or economic well-being of the United States.

children.heading: Not subject to judicial review
children.label: B Not subject to judicial review
children.text: Any objection by the President to an individual serving as an inspector, whether made pursuant to this section or otherwise, shall not be reviewable in any court.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: c Exception
children.text: The requirement under subsection (b)(2)(A) shall not apply to inspections of United States chemical weapons destruction facilities (as used within the meaning of part IV(C)(13) of the Verification Annex to the Convention).
